codeReview.summary: The feature mostly implements passphrase maintenance, but encrypted key replacement does not actually clear or revalidate stale saved passphrase material when the replacement passphrase is not persisted.

issues.description: When an imported encrypted key is replaced and the user does not save the new passphrase, the editor intentionally writes `passphraseStorageState = BLOCKED` (`feature/identities/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/identities/IdentitiesEntryPoint.kt:1007-1014`) and omits a new passphrase secret (`...:1019-1027`). But `RoomIdentityRepository.upsert()` preserves the previous `passphraseCipherText`/`passphraseIv` in that case (`RoomIdentityRepository.kt:97-107`). On the next load, `resolveStorageState()` decrypts that leftover blob and upgrades the passphrase state back to `AVAILABLE`, so the replaced identity can look ready and silently reuse stale passphrase material instead of remaining blocked/repair-needed. This violates the feature contract that key replacement must clear or revalidate stale passphrase state, and the added tests only cover the saved-passphrase replacement path (`app/src/androidTest/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/IdentityPasswordFlowsInstrumentedTest.kt:713-792`), so this regression is not caught.

summary: Fail: replacing an encrypted imported key without persisting the new passphrase can retain the previous encrypted passphrase blob and later surface the identity as passphrase-ready, violating the required stale-passphrase clearing/revalidation behavior.
